title: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
subhed: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.

title: EU gives final approval to €90-billion loan for Ukraine

Uber Eats and Deliveroo accused of human trafficking: 'The situation of delivery workers, the 21st-century proletarians, is outrageous'
subhed: A complaint for human trafficking has been filed in Paris against the food delivery platforms Uber Eats and Deliveroo. This is a global first, observes Le Monde's Eric Albert in his column.

title: 'I was so exhausted, I started hitting myself. They laughed': Four Cuban asylum seekers recount their ordeal at Europe's borders
subhed: The European Court of Human Rights is expected to rule in the coming weeks on the pushbacks experienced by four Cubans at the border between Lithuania and Belarus, against the backdrop of a hybrid war with Russia.

title: Léon Marchand prepares return to French pools: 'I always told myself the Paris Games were just one step'
subhed: The 23-year-old swimmer, who won four Olympic golds in 2024, is set to compete at the French Championships in Saint-Etienne in July, followed by the European Championships in August in Saint-Denis.

title: Boris Pistorius, the man preparing Germany for war
subhed: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who started serving as defense minister under Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
subhed: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.

title: 'Bookshops now serve as sentinels of resistance to the radical rightward shift of society'
subhed: Christian Thorel, a prominent figure in independent book selling, was among the first to sound the alarm about billionaire Vincent Bolloré's ideological grip on the publishing sector, starting with the acquisition of France's top publishing group Hachette, in 2023.

title: Louvre showcases 'beautiful unknown' Martin Schongauer, who influenced Michelangelo and Rembrandt
subhed: An outstanding exhibition brings together around 100 works by the engraver and painter, who was born in Colmar around 1445, all showcasing his exceptional mastery.
